title: poa nemoralis proles glauca
intro: Poa nemoralis proles glauca, also known as the glaucous wood meadow-grass or woodland meadow-grass, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. It can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat various ailments, including skin conditions and respiratory issues.
